title: Tests: HPKE seal/unseal roundtrip and error paths in hpke.rs
description:
What: Test suite for core/src/hpke.rs seal_message/unseal_message using external oracle (hpke crate's own RFC 9180 test vectors + independent key generation).

Algorithm:
1. Read /home/mark/WORK/nie/core/src/hpke.rs (the implementation)
2. Add #[cfg(test)] mod tests to hpke.rs
3. Test 1: roundtrip_basic
   - Generate a random X25519 keypair (StaticSecret::random_from_rng + PublicKey::from)
   - plaintext = b"hello sealed sender"
   - sealed = seal_message(&pub_key_bytes, plaintext)?
   - recovered = unseal_message(&secret_bytes, &sealed)?
   - assert_eq!(recovered, plaintext)
   - Oracle: the seal/unseal inverse property (valid because we're testing the roundtrip, not the algorithm — the hpke crate's own tests validate RFC 9180 vectors)
4. Test 2: wrong_key_fails
   - Generate keypair A and keypair B (different keys)
   - sealed = seal_message(&pub_key_A_bytes, b"secret")?
   - Result = unseal_message(&secret_B_bytes, &sealed)
   - assert!(result.is_err()) — wrong key must return Err, not garbage
5. Test 3: truncated_input_fails
   - sealed = seal_message(&pub_key_bytes, b"data")?
   - Result = unseal_message(&secret_bytes, &sealed[0..10])  // truncated
   - assert!(result.is_err())
6. Test 4: empty_plaintext
   - sealed = seal_message(&pub_key_bytes, b"")?
   - recovered = unseal_message(&secret_bytes, &sealed)?
   - assert_eq!(recovered, b"")
7. Test 5: sealed_size
   - plaintext = b"exactly thirty-two bytes here!!"  // 32 bytes
   - sealed = seal_message(&pub_key_bytes, plaintext)?
   - assert_eq!(sealed.len(), 32 + 32 + 16)  // enc(32) + ciphertext(32) + tag(16) = 80 bytes
   - Documents the wire overhead
8. Run cargo test -p nie-core hpke

Defensive programming rules:
- Tests must NOT use the seal function as the oracle for the unseal function in isolation
- The roundtrip test is valid because both functions are exercised together; correctness of the algorithm is validated by hpke crate's own RFC 9180 vectors
- wrong_key test validates security property: decryption fails with wrong key
- truncated_input test validates bounds checking in unseal_message
- Tests must pass without any internet access (all oracle data is derived locally)
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core hpke passes; 5 tests run; wrong_key and truncated tests verify error conditions
